abstract: Consumers are more easily persuaded by people who are similar to them in looks, behavior, and beliefs. Does similarity’s effect on persuasion extend to similarity in how people sound? We explore how similarity in vocal timbre influences consumer choice. Using machine learning, we generate an objective measure of vocal similarity between an individual consumer and a spokesperson using mel-frequency cepstral coefficients (MFCCs) to capture vocal timbre. First, using data from 7,002 entrepreneur-investor combinations in Shark Tank, we demonstrate the effect of vocal similarity on persuasion in investment pitches. Then, in 2,091 Kickstarter campaigns, we show that a spokesperson’s voice closer to a large audience’s average voice results in higher persuasion, measured by fundraised amount and campaign success – a result driven by vocal similarity. Moreover, these effects are attenuated when external signals of campaign credibility are present. Finally, in four laboratory studies, we show that vocal similarity with a spokesperson or recommender leads to greater trust in their competence and positively influences persuasion. We also show that objective and subjective voice similarity have similar results, with objective similarity mapping on to subjective similarity. We provide a deeper understanding of consumer-spokesperson interactions, including new tools for vocal analytics.

ob.ai_reason: 机器学习提取声音特征预测说服效果
ob.tldr: 研究声音相似性如何影响消费者对代言人的说服效果。采用机器学习提取MFCCs声学特征，分析Shark Tank的7002组投资组合、Kickstarter的2091个众筹项目及4个实验室实验。结果发现声音相似性显著提升说服力，通过信任中介实现；受众平均声音更具说服力；外部信用信号会减弱该效应。研究提供了声音分析的客观测量工具。

title: EXPRESS: Vocal Similarity, Timbre, and Persuasion in Consumer-Spokesperson Interactions
title_zh: EXPRESS：声音相似度、音色与消费者-代言人互动中的说服力
